value: 일본 국외 거주 외국인 여권 소지자. 'Temporary Visitor' 체류자격으로 입국한 자만 구매 가능. 일본 국적자 또는 일본 거주 외국인은 구매 불가.

value.비고: 2023년 10월 인상 후 유지. 2025-2026 추가 인상 없음.

value: 한국 국적자는 90일 이내 관광 목적 무비자 입국 가능. 여권 잔여 유효기간 제한 없음 (체류 기간 이상 권장). Visit Japan Web 사전 등록 권장.
Suica/PASMO 등 IC카드로 전철, 버스, 편의점 결제 가능. 물리 카드 재고 부족은 2025.3월 공식 해소. Welcome Suica(관광객용, 28일 유효) 주요 역에서 판매 재개. 모바일 Suica(Apple/Google) 및 Welcome Suica Mobile(iPhone, 180일 유효) 이용 가능.
지정석(Reserved)과 자유석(Non-reserved) 구분. JR Pass 소지 시 지정석 무료 예약 가능 (Nozomi/Mizuho 제외). 자유석은 선착순 탑승.
면세 범위: 주류 3병(760ml 이하), 담배 200개비(거주자)/400개비(비거주자), 향수 2oz, 기타 품목 20만엔 이하. 초과 시 신고 및 관세 부과.

value.비고: 구급차 무료, 치료비는 별도. 119/110은 일본어 우선이나 통역 연결 가능.

value: Visit Japan Web은 웹 기반 서비스(앱 불필요). 여권 정보, 입국심사(하선카드), 세관신고, 면세쇼핑 3개 항목 사전 등록. 등록 후 QR코드 발급 → 공항 도착 시 제시. 2026년 현재 필수는 아니나 강력 권장 (심사 시간 대폭 단축). 스크린샷 불가, 라이브 접속 필요.

신제도_2026_11월.방식: 환급형 (구매 시 세금 포함 → 출국 시 환급)

value: Tourists can bring in goods for personal use purchased abroad whose total market value is less than 200,000 Yen without paying duties or taxes.

Foreign residents and travelers in Japan can access emergency services by calling 119 for ambulance or fire emergencies; the service can handle languages other than Japanese but having a fluent Japanese speaker helps.
Travel medical insurance in Japan costs approximately $8-10 per day depending on activities and pre-existing conditions and can be purchased after arrival to fit travelers' budgets.
The average price for a cheap hotel room in Kyoto is $79
Visa applicants must physically reside in the jurisdiction of the Consulate General of Japan in San Francisco to apply through them.
Foreigners can use Visit Japan Web for immigration procedures (immigration clearance, customs declaration) and tax-free purchases during their stay in Japan.
¥1,000 for tourists, ¥500 for locals
¥2,500 for tourists, ¥1,000 for locals
¥2,000 climbing fee per person
200 to 300 yen per use
7,600 yen round trip bus from Shinjuku to Mount Fuji 5th station
$240 for 4 days, approximately $40 per day

Suica cards support use on multiple Shinkansen lines including Tōhoku, Hokkaidō, Akita, Yamagata, Jōetsu, and Hokuriku Shinkansen, as well as limited express trains such as Narita Express, Fuji Kaiyū, Azusa, and Kaiji. From November 2025, more limited express trains like Saphir Odoriko will be supported, and from Spring 2026, there will be an option to buy Green Car seats on regular JR East commuter trains.
IC cards require a minimum balance of 1 yen (Icoca), 10 yen (PiTaPa, Sugoca, Nimoca, Hayakaken), or the starting fare amount (Pasmo) to enter ticket gates
The Japan Rail Pass is available only to foreign nationals traveling to Japan as temporary visitors with a 'Temporary Visitor' visa status.
Japanese bakery or kissaten café breakfast: ¥500 to ¥1,000
Fast food chains meals (gyudon, curry, pork cutlet): ¥500–¥800
Set meals / Teishoku: ¥1,000–¥1,500
Izakayas: ¥2,000–¥5,000 per person
Sushi prices range from ¥1,500 to ¥30,000+; conveyor belt sushi plates cost ¥100–¥300 each, with full meals around ¥1,000 to ¥2,500
Mid-range dining: ¥3,000–¥6,000
Osaka street food (takoyaki, kushikatsu): ¥600–¥1,500
Backpacker daily food budget: ¥2,000–¥3,000
Mid-range tourist daily food budget: ¥4,000–¥6,000
nearby Kyoto Station or in downtown Kyoto
Many hotels, especially ryokans, require you to remove your shoes at the entrance or in your room.
Hotel rooms in Japan, especially in Tokyo, tend to be smaller than Western hotels. Business hotels are slightly smaller but effective stays.
09:00–22:30, open year-round including public holidays, no regular days of closure
Last admission 4:00 p.m. for Kondo and Kodo Kanchiin House; last admission 3:30 p.m. for Houmotsukan Museum
Open 6:00AM – 5:00PM; Reception of hand-written sutra offerings: 8:00AM – 5:00PM
Southern Kyoto, near JR Inari Station and Fushimi Inari Station along the Keihan Main Line
Collected info for tips from 40 Best Tips for Visiting Japan in 2025 - by Peter Yang
The SIM card will be automatically activated once you put it into your phone. If asked for the SIM card PIN code, the initial value is '0000'.
SIM cards can be purchased at Haneda Airport Terminal 3 at 7-Eleven (3rd floor), BIC Camera Store, or online as an eSIM at ohayu.com
Airport Wi-Fi in Japan often has time limits, mandatory sign-ups, and heavy congestion during peak arrival hours, making it unreliable for primary connectivity upon arrival.
Many convenience stores and some chain restaurants operate 24 hours a day, even on national holidays.

Kaiseki is Japan’s most refined dining, almost synonymous with Western haute cuisine, requiring the highest level of etiquette and respect for the food, typically prepared by experienced professional chefs.
Naruto Taiyaki Honpo, a chain with locations throughout Japan
Credit cards such as Visa, JCB, and Mastercard are widely accepted in Japan, but travelers should be prepared to pay with cash as an alternative due to possible payment errors.
Suica cards expire 10 years after their last use
Tourist Suica is valid for less than a month
The validity period of a Japan Rail Pass is consecutive 7, 14, or 21 days beginning on the date the pass can actually be first used.
The 10 most popular IC cards, including Suica, Pasmo, and Icoca, are compatible with each other and can be used on almost all train, subway, and bus lines inside Japanese cities.
Japanese public transportation is characterized by its punctuality, superb service, and large crowds of people using it.
Tokyo to Kyoto Shinkansen trip can be covered in just over two hours
Taxi ride from Haneda Airport to Tokyo takes around 30-40 minutes
JR Kansai Airport Rapid Service takes about 50 minutes to Osaka Station
Airport shuttle bus travel time is around 25 minutes to central Osaka
Narita Express train takes 53 minutes to Tokyo Station
Limousine buses from Kansai Airport to Osaka Station take 30 minutes
JR Rapid Airport train from New Chitose Airport to Sapporo takes 37 minutes
Journey time from Narita Airport into Tokyo is approximately 75 minutes
Duty-free allowance for gift and other items except liquor, tobacco and perfume is 200,000 yen (market value). Goods valued under 10,000 yen are exempt from duty charges.

At duty-free shops in Japan, purchases can be made without taxes, but additional taxes apply if purchases exceed the duty-free limit; recording each purchase amount helps to shop within the duty-free limit.
Foreigners are typically required to pay the full cost of urgent treatment upfront unless they hold Japanese public insurance
The multilingual Safety Tips app, overseen by the Japan Tourism Agency, provides alerts about earthquakes and other disasters, as well as evacuation information.
Japan uses many international payment methods such as Apple Pay and Google Pay, but domestic payment methods like PayPay, which allows QR code payments via fast money transfers, are also popular.
If the restaurant doesn’t take reservations and there are people waiting to be seated, try not to linger after you’ve finished eating, especially at ramen or sushi shops.
Japan uses many international payment methods such as Apple Pay and Google Pay, and domestic payment methods like PayPay are popular for QR code payments.

Suica is a rechargeable transportation card from JR recommended for traveling around Japan via train or bus, offering tap-to-pay convenience and integration with travel, shopping, local services, and everyday life.
Overseas visitors can create and top up a digital Suica card before arrival using the official Welcome Suica Mobile App, which was rolled out on March 6, 2025.

From April 1, 2026, Japan Rail Passes reserved via the official JAPAN RAIL PASS Reservation website can be exchanged at select JR East ticket vending machines equipped with passport readers, instead of only at designated ticket offices with live attendants.
Japan Rail Passes reserved via the official JAPAN RAIL PASS Reservation website can be exchanged at select JR East ticket vending machines equipped with passport readers starting April 1, 2026
